name: Mormo
description: Mormo was a female spirit or daemon in Greek mythology, associated with frightening children. She was a terrifying figure, a bogeywoman invoked by nurses and mothers to scare children into behaving. Mormo was often associated with Lamia and Empusa, other monstrous female figures known for preying on the young. She was believed to bite naughty children, and her name was used to frighten them into obedience. While specific myths about Mormo are scarce, her existence as a figure of terror is well-documented in ancient Greek literature and folklore. Mormo represents the primal fear of the unknown and the dangers lurking in the shadows, a cautionary figure used to maintain social order and discipline within families. Her power resided in the fear she instilled, making her a potent symbol of childhood anxieties and parental control. She embodies the dark side of childhood imagination, a creature that haunts the edges of reality and preys on vulnerability. Mormo’s legacy persists as a reminder of the power of fear and the enduring human need to create monsters to explain the inexplicable.
subtitle: The Greek bogeywoman of naughty children
appearance: Mormo's appearance is vague and varies depending on the storyteller. She is generally described as a monstrous, grotesque female figure, possibly with sharp teeth and claws. Her form is often distorted and unsettling, designed to inspire fear. Some accounts suggest she can shapeshift, taking on different terrifying forms to better frighten her victims. She might be depicted with animalistic features or as a shadowy figure lurking in the darkness.
habitat: Mormo is said to dwell in dark places, shadows, and the corners of children's imaginations. She is most active at night, when fear is at its peak. She is not confined to a specific location, but rather exists in the liminal spaces between reality and nightmare, appearing wherever children are vulnerable and susceptible to fear.
behavior: Mormo's primary behavior is to frighten children into obedience. She lurks in the shadows, waiting for an opportunity to torment or even devour naughty children. She is a creature of pure fear, feeding on the anxiety and vulnerability of her victims. Her actions are driven by a desire to instill terror and maintain order through fear. She is often invoked by adults to control children's behavior, making her a tool of parental discipline.

keyMyths.title: Mormo as a Bogeywoman
keyMyths.description: Mormo was primarily known as a bogeywoman used to scare children. Mothers and nurses would invoke her name to frighten children into behaving, threatening them with her appearance if they were disobedient.

sources.author: Unknown
sources.description: Traditional stories and beliefs passed down through generations.
sources.text: Traditional stories and beliefs passed down through generations.
